issues.issueMessage:
Field description: If HED tags are used:
The version of the HED schema used to validate HED tags for study.
May include a single schema or a base schema and one or more library schema.
Field description: Used to specify provenance of the dataset.
Format "number" must be datetime

Field description: Object containing key-value pairs related to the software used to present
the stimuli during the experiment.

Field description: Key-value pairs of the labels and 3-D digitized position of anatomical
landmarks, interpreted following the `"FiducialsCoordinateSystem"`
(for example, `{"NAS": [12.7,21.3,13.9], "LPA": [5.2,11.3,9.6],
"RPA": [20.2,11.3,9.1]}`).
Each array MUST contain three numeric values corresponding to x, y, and z
axis of the coordinate system in that exact order.

Field description: Defines the coordinate system for the fiducials.
Preferably the same as the `"EEGCoordinateSystem"`.
See the
[Coordinate Systems Appendix](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/coordinate-systems.md)
for a list of restricted keywords for coordinate systems.
If `"Other"`, provide definition of the coordinate system in
`"FiducialsCoordinateSystemDescription"`.

Field description: Units in which the coordinates that are  listed in the field
`"FiducialsCoordinateSystem"` are represented.

Field description: Free-form text description of the coordinate system.
May also include a link to a documentation page or paper describing the
system in greater detail.

Field description: Key-value pairs of the labels and 3-D digitized locations of anatomical landmarks,
interpreted following the `"AnatomicalLandmarkCoordinateSystem"`
(for example, `{"NAS": [12.7,21.3,13.9], "LPA": [5.2,11.3,9.6],
"RPA": [20.2,11.3,9.1]}`.
Each array MUST contain three numeric values corresponding to x, y, and z
axis of the coordinate system in that exact order.

Field description: Defines the coordinate system for the anatomical landmarks.
See the [Coordinate Systems Appendix](SPEC_ROOT/appendices/coordinate-systems.md)
for a list of restricted keywords for coordinate systems.
If `"Other"`, provide definition of the coordinate system in
`"AnatomicalLandmarkCoordinateSystemDescription"`.
Field description: Units of the coordinates of `"AnatomicalLandmarkCoordinateSystem"`.

Field description: Free-form text description of the coordinate system.
May also include a link to a documentation page or paper describing the
system in greater detail.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: The serial number of the equipment that produced the measurements.
A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [Cognitive Atlas](https://www.cognitiveatlas.org/)
Task term.

Field description: [URI](SPEC_ROOT/common-principles.md#uniform-resource-indicator)
of the corresponding [CogPO](http://www.cogpo.org/) term.
Field description: Name of the cap manufacturer (for example, `"EasyCap"`).

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of the cap model
(for example, `"actiCAP 64 Ch Standard-2"`).
Field description: Number of EMG channels.
Field description: Number of miscellaneous channels not covered otherwise.

Field description: Number of channels for digital (binary TTL) triggers or analog equivalents (TTL in volt).
Corresponds to the `TRIG` channel type.

Field description: Description of the location of the ground electrode
(for example, `"placed on right mastoid (M2)"`).
Field description: Circumference of the participant's head, expressed in cm (for example, `58`).

Field description: Placement scheme of EEG electrodes.
Either the name of a standardized placement system (for example, `"10-20"`)
or a list of standardized electrode names (for example, `["Cz", "Pz"]`).

Field description: Object of temporal hardware filters applied, or `"n/a"` if the data is not
available. Each key-value pair in the JSON object is a name of the filter and
an object in which its parameters are defined as key-value pairs.
For example, `{"Highpass RC filter": {"Half amplitude cutoff (Hz)":
0.0159, "Roll-off": "6dB/Octave"}}`.

Field description: Freeform description of the observed subject artifact and its possible cause
(for example, `"Vagus Nerve Stimulator"`, `"non-removable implant"`).
If this field is set to `"n/a"`, it will be interpreted as absence of major
source of artifacts except cardiac and blinks.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.

Field description: The serial number of the equipment that produced the measurements.
A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.

Field description: Can be used if all electrodes are of the same manufacturer
(for example, `"AD-TECH"`, `"DIXI"`).
If electrodes of different manufacturers are used,
please use the corresponding table in the `_electrodes.tsv` file.

Field description: If different electrode types are used,
please use the corresponding table in the `_electrodes.tsv` file.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.

Field description: Object of temporal hardware filters applied, or `"n/a"` if the data is not
available. Each key-value pair in the JSON object is a name of the filter and
an object in which its parameters are defined as key-value pairs.
For example, `{"Highpass RC filter": {"Half amplitude cutoff (Hz)":
0.0159, "Roll-off": "6dB/Octave"}}`.

Field description: The serial number of the equipment that produced the measurements.
A pseudonym can also be used to prevent the equipment from being
identifiable, so long as each pseudonym is unique within the dataset.
Field description: Manufacturer's model name of the equipment that produced the measurements.

Field description: Manufacturer's designation of software version of the equipment that produced
the measurements.
Field description: Text of the instructions given to participants before the recording.
Field description: Number of acceleration channels.
Field description: Number of angular acceleration channels.
Field description: Number of gyrometer channels.
Field description: Number of joint angle channels.
Field description: Number of Latency channels.
Field description: Number of magnetometer channels.
Field description: Number of miscellaneous channels not covered otherwise.

Field description: Describes how missing values are represented in the given recording system
(for example a tracking system in motion), can take values such as, "NaN", "0".
Field description: Number of orientation channels.

Field description: Effective sampling frequency (in Hz) of all the data in the recording,
regardless of their type (for example, `2400`) which can be determined if timestamps
per sample are provided.

Field description: Freeform description of the observed subject artifact and its possible cause
(for example, `"Vagus Nerve Stimulator"`, `"non-removable implant"`).
If this field is set to `"n/a"`, it will be interpreted as absence of major
source of artifacts except cardiac and blinks.
Field description: Number of different tracked points tracked in a motion tracking system.
Field description: Number of linear velocity channels.

codeMessages.JSON_KEY_RECOMMENDED: A JSON file is missing a key listed as recommended.
codeMessages.TSV_COLUMN_TYPE_REDEFINED: A column required in a TSV file has been redefined in a sidecar file. This redefinition is being ignored.
codeMessages.SIDECAR_KEY_RECOMMENDED: A data file's JSON sidecar is missing a key listed as recommended.
codeMessages.TSV_ADDITIONAL_COLUMNS_UNDEFINED: A TSV file has extra columns which are not defined in its associated JSON sidecar
